title: Angular Data Binding – Connecting Logic and UI

meta.title: Angular Data Binding Guide – Interpolation, Property & Two-Way Binding (2026)
meta.description: Learn the four types of data binding in Angular. Master how to sync data between your TypeScript components and HTML templates efficiently.

content.value: Data binding is a core feature of Angular that coordinates the communication between a component's TypeScript class (the logic) and its HTML template (the view). As a Technical Lead, you know that efficient data flow is the backbone of a responsive UI. Angular provides four distinct types of binding, categorized by the direction of data flow.
This type of binding pushes data from the TypeScript class into the HTML template. It is used to display dynamic content or set element properties.

value: **Interpolation `{{ value }}`**: Used to render text content within HTML tags.
**Property Binding `[property]="value"`**: Used to set the property of an element or a directive (e.g., [disabled], [src], [hidden]).

content.value: This binding handles user interactions, such as clicks, keystrokes, and mouse movements, by calling methods in your TypeScript class.

value: **Event Binding `(event)="method()"`**: Listens for specific DOM events and triggers logic in the component.

content.value: Two-way binding allows for a continuous synchronization of data between the view and the source. If the user changes a value in an input field, the TypeScript variable updates; if the variable is updated via logic, the input field reflects the change.

value: **Two-Way Binding `[(ngModel)]="value"`**: Commonly used in forms. It combines property binding and event binding syntax (the 'banana in a box').

faq.question: What is the 'Banana in a box' syntax?
faq.answer: It refers to the [( )] syntax used for two-way data binding. An easy way to remember the order of brackets is 'the banana (parentheses) goes inside the box (square brackets)'.

faq.question: Why use [property] instead of {{ }} for attributes?
faq.answer: Interpolation always converts the value to a string. Property binding [ ] allows you to pass non-string values like booleans or objects directly to elements or components.

faq.question: Does ngModel require a specific module?
faq.answer: Yes. To use [(ngModel)], you must import 'FormsModule' from '@angular/forms' into your Angular Module (or standalone component).

quiz.question: Which syntax is used for property binding in Angular?
What is the primary use case for Event Binding?
options: Displaying a variable in the UI
Handling user interactions like button clicks
Passing an object to a child component
